response:
the engineer models the query response time for each database as a function of the number of queries q it handles  
the total query response time function t_total(q) is the sum of the individual response times for each database  
the engineer needs to find the optimal distribution of queries among the three databases to minimize the total response time  
the total query response time function t_total(q) is the sum of the individual response times for each database  

the mysql response time is a * q^2 + b * e^(c * q)  
the oracle response time is d * ln(q + 1) + e / (q + 1)  
the sybase response time is f * q^(3/2) - g * sqrt(q)  
the total response time is the sum of the three individual response times  

the total response time function is t_total(q) = a * (alpha * q)^2 + b * e^(c * (alpha * q)) + d * ln(q + 1) + e / (q + 1) + f * (q - alpha * q)^(3/2) - g * sqrt(q - alpha * q)  

the optimal values of alpha and beta are those that minimize the total response time function t_total(q)  
the optimal values of alpha and beta are those that minimize the total response time function t_total(q)
